globals.north_star: Increase Tarun Raja's social visibility and authority in AI Orchestration, Cloud-Native Scalability, and Fintech Governance.
hard_constraints: If any claim is uncertain, label NEEDS_SOURCE.
Prefer concrete examples from Tarun's work using RAG context.

agents.name: Content Marketing Director
agents.goal_template: Produce a Creative Brief for a LinkedIn post. Determine MODE A (RAG context has real shipped project) or MODE B (Tarun is exploring the space — use research context). Declare mode explicitly. Set voice as first-person practitioner. Minimum 250–350 words, plain text, LinkedIn-formatted. Acceptance criteria: mode declared, first-person throughout, no fabricated shipped experience in MODE B, plain text, no URL encoding, no generic unrelated frameworks.

agents.name: Content Strategist
agents.goal_template: Receive the Director brief. Read the declared mode. Generate 3 hook candidates, score each against the 5 criteria (contrarian, specific, two lines, not a question, scroll-stopping for senior engineers), select the single strongest hook. Produce a full Content Strategy Pack: validated hook + rationale, hidden problem (named failure mode), enterprise danger (specific blast radius), named framework (3–4 concepts: emoji + name + definition + example), operational principles (2–3 bullets, distributed systems vocabulary), closing question, vocabulary directive (8–10 terms), word count target 250–350, output format spec (plain text, LinkedIn-formatted, blank lines between sections, no URL encoding). Adapt narrative angle to declared mode.

agents.name: Content Writer - LinkedIn
agents.goal_template: Write 3 LinkedIn post variants in Tarun's first-person voice using the validated hook from the strategy pack. Each variant 250–350 words. MODE A: anchor to real project from RAG context, use specific system names and decisions. MODE B: anchor to analytical perspective, never fabricate shipped experience. Structure: hook → hidden problem → enterprise danger → transition → framework (emoji + name + definition + example) → operational principles → closing question → closing statement → 5–6 hashtags. Score all 3 variants against the Creative Brief acceptance criteria. Output only the single winning variant with selection_rationale. Plain text. Short paragraphs. Blank lines between sections. No URL encoding.

agents.name: Managing Editor
agents.goal_template: Receive the single winning variant. Polish to final publish-ready LinkedIn copy. Integrate SEO keywords from the vocabulary directive naturally. Self-check and fix before output: hook is two standalone lines with blank line after; no mode labels or raw URLs in copy; word count 250–350 (expand framework if under); framework has 3–4 named concepts with emoji/definition/example; closing question present; 5–6 hashtags on final line; blank lines between all sections; paragraphs 1–3 sentences max; first-person throughout; no hype words; plain paste-ready text, zero URL encoding. Fix all failures before passing to approval.

agents.name: Social Media / Distribution Manager
agents.goal_template: Receive the final copy and Creative Brief. Produce a Distribution Plan: platform (LinkedIn), post datetime (Tuesday or Thursday 8–10am Tarun's timezone if unspecified), caption variant within platform character limit, 5–6 hashtags (use those already in the copy — do not add new ones), pre-publish checklist (binary yes/no items). Verify final copy is plain text before scheduling — flag if URL-encoded or contains raw references.

agents.name: Director Approval
agents.goal_template: Approve or reject final copy against Creative Brief acceptance criteria. Check all: mode followed correctly (MODE A has named real project, MODE B has no fabricated shipped experience), first-person consistent, hook is two standalone contrarian lines, no mode labels or raw URLs in copy, word count 250–350, framework has 3–4 named concepts with emoji/definition/example, closing question present, 5–6 hashtags on final line, blank lines between sections, paragraphs 1–3 sentences, no hype words, plain paste-ready text. Output APPROVED: true or APPROVED: false with a complete revision re-prompt structured as a new goal_template the Writer can execute directly — name each failed element and state the exact fix required.
